transcript.text: Hi.
Hey, how are you?
Good, how are you?
Good, good.
Nice to see you at the office.
Oh, yeah.
It's the life.
Are you home or are in an office?
You're the only one there, right, I assume?
You're working from home all the time.
Yeah, yeah, I'm working from home.
Sometimes I go to a co-working space, but yeah, like I switch back and forth basically when I get sick of my bedroom.
You're interested perfect, by the way.
Did you ever live in the U.S.?
I did, yeah.
I have family in Arizona and in SF.
Okay.
I lived in Tennessee for a year.
I was an exchange student, so I've around.
Tennessee will help you hone in that.
will be super American.
Exactly.
Yeah, I remember when I came back.
And I, I mean, my accent was just very, very Southern.
I'm one of those people I, first of all, if I'm around people who speak English with an accent, I adopt their accent because I'm just, I just mimic people like subconsciously.
And my Tennessee accent was very, very country.
That's cool.
Yeah, yeah, it's great.
Before we dive in, I got to ask how that Nine Inch Nails concert was.
Oh, yeah.
It was incredible.
Super hot for some reason.
The venue, they didn't want to turn on the AC, but just when you ignore that, it was great.
Or the experience, guess, yeah.
Yeah, I mean, was like, Trent Reznor is like 60, but the energy is just, I mean, he's really, really, so really high level.
And it was a great performance.
Like, it exceeded my expectations.
So, yeah, he should definitely go if he comes to California again, which I'm sure he will.
So, yeah.
Yeah, will, yeah.
Is 60?
That surprised me.
That's so sad.
He also has five kids, which I did not know.
Five?
Five, yeah.
I was like, no wonder he's been touring, he has five kids.
Yeah, it's probably easier to tour.
Yeah, yeah.
All right, well, Marcel might join us later.
They're filming something at the office, so he might pop in, but he gave us the go-ahead to get started.
So I'm just going to share my screen real quick with the agenda.
Yep.
I've got it pulled up too.
Yeah, so just to kind of recap the progress so far.
So last week, no, this week actually, Monday, I sent over two calibration blogs, which I believe are all, like, have mostly been reviewed.
Is there anyone else that we're waiting on?
No.
No?
You clearly haven't gone in the dark in the last...
Okay, so here's the deal.
So Ryan and Anisha and one person that attacks two looked at it high level.
So the good news is from a tax content perspective, it's like quite accurate.
We felt good about it.
That's good.
And then from a voice perspective, Anisha didn't, thought it was a little too like technical and not quite as like, so she, she didn't make the line edits.
I think she sent you the prompt, right?
That the kind of tweaks that could be made.

And then I asked one person from the tax team, Caroline, like Ryan and I asked her to go do a fine grained, like, look at the article for like, like the most detail oriented, like critical version of it.
A true CPA review.
Yeah.
Yeah.
You need to give me access because I can't see her comments.
Oh, shoot.
If you look at the docs, she's probably going to have, like, 50 edits in each one of them, okay?
Yeah, that's totally fine, yeah.
Yeah, and so I think, like, it's for little things, like, the one example is I think one of the docs uses the word write-off a lot, but technically it's, like, a deduction.
And I don't think for, like, piece of content, 74, we're going to care that much.
But I think for the first couple, we want to make sure that, like, no one can look at them and be like, oh, it's wrong or it's, like, slightly off.
So she's finishing that edit now, and then I'm going to do – I'm going to read it one more time with some more tweaks.
So now we're not going to do that for every blog because – I mean, you're saving us a ton of time, but it would still probably take a little too much time.
But I wanted one version where you see, like, what the perfect human version of the content would be, just if it's helpful for the AI.
I'll be done with that either tonight or tomorrow.
Okay.
Okay.
And then after that, you know, it's kind of good to go.
Okay.
Perfect.
Well, I mean, the comments are totally fine and expected.
And like, we can very easily take that feedback and then tweak the writing guidelines.
I mean, that's kind of, it's what we're trying to do with the calibration blogs.
And yeah, I've been, I've been writing for a long time.
So I'm very used to like going into the Google doc and that there being like a hundred comments.
So I'm all good with that.
But yeah, I saw that she was in the doc.
So that's why I asked.
Okay.
That sounds good.
Then the next step would be also, so sometime this morning, like doing some additional like keyword research around your audience pain points.
Um, and I added them to the content OS, um, to the considering status.
So when you get the chance, um, this week, you can go.
Bye.

And basically our goal for this week is try to get like try to approve a batch of content and by a batch I mean like 20, 30 like depending on obviously if you don't feel like there's a like there's a batch of content for you to approve you can let me know and we'll like find some additional topics but you basically just go in here and then like you go to the status and just change it to approve to start to start that yeah so it should be pretty pretty straightforward i can do that again by tomorrow morning i can have 20 20 in there you want 20 right that's the ideal number 20 to 30 like i mean i'm not gonna be bad there are more so okay cool i'll do that okay perfect thank you um and then uh okay so i'm gonna tweak the writing guidelines okay so this week we tweaked the writing guidelines we get a batch of content approved and basically next week basically next

The goal would be to kind of scale production and I was like wanting to ask you what do you think like how do you want us to ramp up do you want us to just go full blown like seven pieces next week or like do you want to start maybe with a smaller batch like three to four as you figure out because from my experience working with clients it takes like the content reviews someone still wants to like usually clients they want to read the blogs and that takes time.
So I just want to like hear if you have someone in your team that's like gonna own this or what were you thinking?
Actually we haven't talked about how you're gonna do it initially.
I think I'm probably gonna want to read the first 10 anyways and then the one person from the tax team.
So I think that means it'll take us two days to review because the person texting will probably take them 30 minutes.
It'll probably take me like 30 minutes to just like make edits and approve.
So...
So that probably means we can do two to three a week, I think, for the first, like, couple weeks.
And after that, as we get comfortable, we can probably speed that up.
What would you, like, do you think we need to have one piece of content a day?
Like, what, where do we, yeah, what's the end game that you think is good for us?
Yeah, great question.
So typically we like to, for, I the clients that I've worked with in the past, we typically, like, deliver on a weekly basis.
So once a week, we'll notify you that, like, you have a new batch of content that's ready for you in Airtable for your review.
And depending on the client, that's usually, like, it can be between, like, five to seven pieces of content a week.
It also depends on the length, because, you know, if we do, like, three articles that are, like, 3,000 words long each, that's basically, like, six blogs that are, you know, half that length.
So I would like us to get to at least.
Five pieces per week.
Okay.
All right.
Let's do this.
Let's do the two this week.
Let's do two next week and just so we figure out on our side how we can streamline.
Then let's up that to four for the week after.
And then if that's good, then we'll try to go to five.
And then just to step back, my understanding here is like we're doing blog content for like a month or so to learn, right, from the analytics and all those things.

And then we're going to step back and we're going to like, based on what we've learned, we're going to decide a little bit more what we focus the blog on, what we focus landing pages on, what we focus the content library on.
But we wanted to gather the data early on.
Is that correct?
Yeah.
I mean, for a lot of the clients that we work with, they, you know, used like the first, maybe the first six weeks is when.

You get enough data to make a decision like what kind of content lane we want to focus on, if there's like a specific vertical that you want to like pour most of the resources into so that we can definitely do that.

And I didn't forget that in our kickoff call, you mentioned that you would like us to also come up with like something that's really interesting or like an experiment that's kind of like not just SEO content.
So I haven't forgotten that.
I just need to, like, I can't make any suggestions right now.
I have to meet with a dev team and speak to Marcel.
They'll just understand like what their capacity is right now.
Totally.
Yeah.
We've got like six months.
Yeah.
It's not like tomorrow.
Okay.
Okay.
So does that work for you?
So I'll approve the two this week, like by tomorrow, and then you'll send us a couple more next week.
We'll review that, and then we'll go to four, and then we'll see from there, and then Ryan and I will figure out how to operationalize it on our end.
Mm-hmm.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Sounds good.
I mean, the...
The clients that I've worked with so far, they start seeing impressions and clicks, like within, usually within a week after publishing, or at least like two weeks max.

You are a new domain, but I have kind of, I mean, I've incorporated a lot of like very low volume, very low, like not as very competitive keywords in the content OS, so we should like have a good balance between like content that you should have on your website, because it helps to build authority, and also content that you can actually rank with in Google, like on page one.
Okay, cool.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Okay.
Perfect.
Have access to your GSC, GA4, so we're all good on that front.
Okay.
Perfect.
So in terms of images, I don't know if we've actually discussed this, so.
For the businesses that we work with, they usually choose between stock images, or AI-generated illustrations, or AI-generated photos that kind of look like stock images.
And I was looking at different audience competitors that we have, and a lot of them use stock images.
Because Bench does something different, so they have, like, these, like, pretty generic SaaS illustrations, but they do fit with their website, and I do prefer them to stock images.
So I'm just wondering, like, what your thoughts are on that.
I don't like illustrations, actually.
Okay.
But I hate stock images, too.
So this is something we need Anisha for, honestly.
We, we're, we're actually about to do a photo shoot for our website, for the new website.
I think we...

We decided on images, we have like guidelines on the kind of images that we want, I think we're totally fine using AI generated images, but they'll need to be in a certain style, it shouldn't be too difficult to generate them.
But basically, it's like, if you go to square.com right now.

Yeah, so go see how I have all these, but it feels like red, it feels like red people, real people, like if you click on any business type, like click on at the bottom of their business types, click on any of them.
Yeah.
All right.
Yeah.
Yeah.
It's like, it feels like real, like a real business.
So it's a stock.
We've, we think with AI, we could generate at scale things that look like a real business owner doing something or real, like, that's what we want, you know, know, um, but we define.

Finding it takes a little bit of work, so what you need from us, this is when you need to have a call with Anisha, or I need to, let me see if she's free right now actually, I think she's remote, let me see if I can dial her in.
I don't know if she's around, if she's around, I'll add her to the meeting, but the, like the reality is, I think AI generated images with a certain style is probably the way we would want to go.
Okay, yeah, I mean, we have done, I think for home base, I'm not sure.
So...
So...
So...

The cases right now, but they were using like AI generated images that are quite similar to this, like featuring real people or maybe let me let me let me actually just go to their site and where's the blog.
Yeah, exactly.
Like the how to do payroll in South Dakota.
Yeah, yeah.
Like that dude, like that's...
Yeah, yeah.
These are exactly, that's exactly, I mean, yeah, exactly.
That super works for us.
Yeah, yeah.
it's kind of like, I don't know, stock images, look very sterile.
Like there's a very...
Yes.
The, it's, yeah.
yeah.
You want something that looks, like, more realistic and human.
That's right.

Like, I don't want, you know, when you have a picture of an accountant, I just, I don't want, like, five 27-year-old dudes in suits in a glass office, you know, that looks like Goldman Sachs, but, like, the picture there of the, you know, the woman coming out of the florist or, like, the two restaurant owners, like, that's perfect.
Like, that's exactly the kind of thing we're trying to do.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
Yeah, that's great to, I mean, yeah, it's great to have for inspiration.

I think when we, like, start publishing and we would want to get publishing, like, as soon as possible, I think we can, like, have, maybe generate some of this stuff in mid-journey and then once you have, like, a more, like, more defined guidelines internally, we can easily go back and replace them.
So, because I know that they were using the journey to do home-based.

This stuff for a while, but I know that there are also, like, in Atlas, are also image generation workflows that use different APIs, so they use, they don't use Midjourney, but they use other AI image generators that do a really good job.
So we could also build, like, a workflow to generate these images at scale.
Okay, nice.
All right, great.
So that's out of the way.
And then the last thing I wanted to talk about was the blog part.
So the web developer has a couple of questions for you.
So he has access to Framer, and he was going to start working tomorrow, and he was wondering if there's a reason why the seed announcement was set up as a page.
No, laziness.
Laziness.
Okay.
So the answer is laziness, and no, should, yeah, you should convert the seed announcement to a CMS entry.
Maybe just make sure the slug doesn't change.
Maybe doesn't Thank you.
Like, make sure the old slug continues to work.
And then I would do a simple format layout on the blog homepage for the current website.
Once we have the new website, I'll ask Tony and whoever's working on it to make sure that we design the blog page.
And maybe we will reach out to you about that to make, you know what I mean?
To make sure it's a good design.
Tony's out this week, but I'll put it on his plate on Monday.
And he'll, he's like my co-founder, but he's a designer.
And he'll, he can work with you on making sure we have a good format layout for the blog and on the new website that we're launching in August.
Okay.
Okay.
So I'll just, yeah, I'll tell, I'll tell Ed to just make it like super simple and then we can tweak it later.
Okay, great.
So last thing I wanted to talk about.
So we are in week four.
I was just wondering, like, do have any feedback so far?
Or like.
Like, how do you feel about the collaboration and if there's anything that you were hoping you would get out of us by this point that we haven't delivered?
No, it's felt good.
Like, I mean, I thought that honestly the two pieces of content were quite good.
Like, I was quite impressed by the level of detail.
If anything, I thought they were too long and too detailed, like, which is good for the first two, like, but I'm very excited about the roadmap.
I think getting content out ASAP and getting data is the right thing to do for the next four to six weeks.
As soon as we're done with that, I want to step back and be a little more strategic and decide a little bit, like, where exactly do we want to play?
How do we get, like, this, like, content library for text in a good place?
What are the right verticals to go after?
But otherwise, I feel very good about the relationship.
That's I mean, there's, like...
So
So you're saving us a huge amount of time, right, for a really high-quality product.
So we're super happy.
Okay, perfect.
Thank you.
Yeah, I think that's definitely, like, the way to go.
I think you're going to have a lot of, like, I don't want to, like, jump, like, make any assumptions too soon.
But I think you're going to do really well in the content that targets, like, individual, like, small business verticals.
So anything related to dentists, e-commerce, real estate, those are, like, very not as competitive and we can really, like, do a good job there.
I had a similar client.
They're a dental staffing app and their website wasn't, like, super high authority or anything.
They didn't have a blog, so we kind of built it from scratch and we saw a lot of good results there by, like, targeting stuff that was very, very, like, low difficulty, not as much competition.
But there's a real intent.
And behind those searches.
Cool.
Yeah.
That is a high thesis.
I hope it turns out to be true.
Are there things on our end we can do better that would make your life easier?
No, I think usually most common issue that I run into is just people being unresponsive.
And you've been really responsive, so that's great.
No other notes for me.
Okay.
Yeah, I'll be responsive.
Sometimes I just don't have the time, but I'm trying my best to make the time.
And I think getting in the cadence where we're doing like four to five pieces of content a week, like we'll figure out how to get that going, but I'm excited about it.
Okay, great.
And another question.
So I spoke to Anjum for the startup related content.
Is there someone who's a good choice for like some of the more small business or content?
you, Anjum Thank
Yes, they're like Jess or Caroline, Jess?
Okay, I'll connect you with Jess.
Ryan or I will connect you with someone to work on that.
Yeah, okay, perfect.
It's just good to have those two clusters handled.
Yeah, we have a couple of people on the team, but we'll find someone and then connect them with you and you can either meet with them or do it by email or whatever is easier.
Okay, great.
Do you guys have any other questions?
I'm all wrapped up from my end.
No, I think what I've got on my to-do list is I'm going to approve the current content, look at the potential list of future topics and get 30 plus with a thumbs up.
Yeah.
I'll make sure Tony reaches out about design for the new website at some point, but in the meantime, we all can
Do what you think is right.
I will ask Anisha to connect with you on images, and then we'll find someone on task to do small business content.
Yes, and just read through the two calibration blogs that you mentioned earlier.
Okay.
Yeah, that was the first thing.
Okay, great.
Well, thank you so much for your time.
We'll stay in touch on Slack.
Perfect.
Sounds good.
Awesome.
Thank you.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between GrowthX and Town to discuss content strategy progress and next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=2.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content calibration blogs reviewed; minor tweaks needed for technical accuracy and voice](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=159.0)
  - [Ramping up content production: 2 pieces/week initially, scaling to 5/week over next month](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=498.0)
  - [AI-generated images preferred; aiming for realistic, relatable visuals (similar to Square.com)](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=714.0)
  - [Blog setup on Framer to be simplified initially, with design improvements planned for August](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1082.0)

## Topics

### Content Calibration and Review Process

  - [Two calibration blogs sent Monday; reviewed by Ryan, Anisha, and tax team](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=164.0)
  - [Content accuracy good; voice needs adjustment (less technical, more conversational)](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=198.0)
  - [Detailed CPA review in progress; \~50 edits expected per piece](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=213.0)
  - [Final review by Market (SF Office) tonight/tomorrow](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=239.0)
  - [Writing guidelines to be tweaked based on feedback](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=311.0)

### Content Production Ramp-up

  - [Week 1-2: 2 pieces/week](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=545.0)
  - [Week 3: 4 pieces/week](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=551.0)
  - [Week 4+: Aim for 5 pieces/week (1000-1500 words each)](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=489.0)
  - [First 6 weeks: Gather data to inform content strategy](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=558.0)
  - [Market (SF Office) to read first 10 pieces; tax team to review (30 min each)](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=463.0)

### Image Strategy for Blog Posts

  - [AI-generated images preferred over stock photos or illustrations](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [Aiming for realistic, relatable visuals (similar to Square.com)](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=736.0)
  - [Short-term: Use Midjourney for initial images](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1016.0)
  - [Long-term: Develop custom AI workflow for scaled image generation](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)
  - [Anisha to provide detailed image guidelines](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)

### Blog Setup and Design

  - [Convert seed announcement to CMS entry (maintain old slug)](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1069.0)
  - [Implement simple blog homepage layout for current website](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1082.0)
  - [Tony (co-founder/designer) to work on new blog design for August website launch](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1090.0)

### Content Strategy and Feedback

  - [Focus on individual small business verticals (e.g., dentists, e-commerce, real estate)](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1220.0)
  - [Target low-difficulty, high-intent keywords](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1229.0)
  - [Positive feedback on collaboration and time-saving aspects](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1193.0)
  - [Connect with Jess/Caroline for small business content expertise](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1324.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Market (SF Office) to approve current content and 30+ future topics](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1356.0)
  - [Review and incorporate feedback from calibration blog edits](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=245.0)
  - [Implement simple blog layout; await Tony's design input for August launch](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1082.0)
  - [Anisha to connect regarding image guidelines](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)
  - [GrowthX to start producing 2 content pieces/week, scaling up gradually](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=551.0)
  - [Market (SF Office) to connect GrowthX with small business content expert (Jess/Caroline)](https://fathom.video/share/izJy9yaSXWSo8S2sR6oeKRPKdv5TPdyZ?tab=summary&timestamp=1324.0)

action_items.description: Review 2 calibration blogs w/ detailed edits from Caroline
Approve 2 current content pieces + select 30+ future topics in Airtable
Have Anisha connect w/ Aida re image guidelines for AI-gen photos
Connect Aida w/ Jess or Caroline for small business content expertise
